items.title: US Stocks To Open Higher Ahead Of Fed's Decision This Week: Analyst Says 'Santa Comes Later In December'
items.author: Rishabh Mishra
items.description: U.S. stock futures are trading higher ahead of the Federal Reserve's decision this week, with the Nasdaq 100 index setting new record highs. Analysts expect a 25 basis point rate cut, and the market is anticipating a potential 'Santa Rally' in the latter half of December.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The Nasdaq 100 index, which the QQQ ETF tracks, has been setting new record highs, indicating strong performance in the tech-heavy index.
U.S. stock futures, including the S&P 500 index which the SPY ETF tracks, are trading higher in premarket, suggesting a positive outlook for the broader market.
Broadcom's strong quarterly earnings and upbeat AI-driven guidance have propelled the company into the exclusive $1 trillion market-cap club, indicating a positive outlook for the company.
Tesla's stock price is up in premarket trading, with Wedbush analyst Dan Ives citing the company's AI and autonomous opportunity as a positive factor.
Amplitech Group's stock is up in premarket trading after the company announced the sale of 1,352,500 shares of its common stock, indicating a positive development for the company.

Checkpoint Therapeutics' stock is up in premarket trading after the FDA approved the company's UnloxcyT for the treatment of adults with metastatic cutaneous squamous cell carcinoma, a positive development for the company.
